_templateName: Build a Telegram Chatbot with Gemini
_templateDescription:
Overview
This workflow connects a Telegram bot to OpenAi/Google Gemini (PaLM API) so the bot can reply to users with AI-generated answers.
Useful for FAQs, assistants, classroom helpers, or bots that fetch document content to answer questions.

Who is this for
Educators, creators, developers, and support teams who want a low-code Telegram chatbot powered by Gemini.

What it does (quick)
Listens for messages sent to your Telegram bot.
Sends incoming text to Google Gemini and receives a generated reply.
Optionally fetches content from Google Docs or an external API to enrich replies.
Sends the reply back to the original Telegram user.
Processes messages in batches and adds short delays to avoid spamming.

Quick setup (5 steps)
Create a Telegram bot with @BotFather and copy the bot token.  
Add Telegram credentials to n8n (Telegram node).  
OpenAi API key and add it to n8n.
Get a Google Gemini (PaLM) API key and add it to n8n.  
(Optional) Connect Google Docs OAuth2 if you want the bot to read documents.  
Activate the workflow and test by messaging the bot.

Required items
Telegram bot token
OpenAi API key
Google Gemini (PaLM) API key
n8n instance with Telegram and HTTP nodes enabled
(Optional) Google Docs OAuth2 credential

How it works (step-by-step)
Telegram message arrives → Trigger node.  
Workflow extracts message and user info.  
(Optional) Pull supporting content from Google Docs or an API.  
Send prompt + context to Gemini → receive reply.  
Send reply back to the Telegram user.  
Add small delays and batch processing to handle volume safely.

How to customize
Edit the Gemini prompt to change response style and behavior.  
OpenAi Chat model
Switch Gemini model (Flash vs Pro) for speed vs. quality.  
Add conditions (If / Switch) to route different inputs to different behaviors.  
Append more data sources (Sheets, external APIs) to enrich replies.  
Add error handling to retry or log failed requests.

Testing checklist
Send a test message to the bot and confirm a reply.  
If using Google Docs, confirm the bot can read the target document.  
Check logs and node outputs in n8n for any errors.

Tips and best practices
Keep prompts concise and include only needed context to reduce costs.  
Use rate limiting (Wait node) and batching to avoid API throttling.  
Store API keys securely in n8n credentials.  
Start with small tests before enabling automated production runs.

🙋 For Help & Community
👾 Discord: n8n channel
🌐 Website: devcodejourney.com
🔗 LinkedIn: Connect with Shakil
📱 WhatsApp Channel: Join Now
💬 Direct Chat: Message Now

nodes.name: Get a document in Google Docs
Message a model in Google Gemini

parameters.content:
# 🤖 Telegram + Gemini + Google Docs Automation in n8n

## 📌 Overview

This n8n workflow listens for messages sent to a Telegram bot and replies with AI-generated responses using **Google Gemini (PaLM)**. It optionally interacts with **Google Docs** to fetch documents and makes external API requests using an **HTTP Request** node.

The flow also includes batching and waiting logic to control the pace of replies, making it scalable and human-friendly.

---

## 🧠 What This Workflow Does

### ✅ 1. Trigger from Telegram
- **Node**: `Telegram Trigger`
- **Function**: Starts the workflow when a message is received via your Telegram bot.

### ✅ 2. Loop Through Messages
- **Node**: `Loop Over Items`
- **Function**: Allows processing multiple incoming messages if required, using batching logic.

### ✅ 3. AI-Powered Reply with Gemini
- **Node**: `Message a model` (Google Gemini Pro)
- **Function**: Sends the message to the Gemini AI (via Google PaLM API) to generate a human-friendly, conversational response.

### ✅ 4. Send AI Response via Telegram
- **Node**: `Send a text message`
- **Function**: Sends the generated Gemini response back to the user on Telegram.

### ✅ 5. Delay Between Replies
- **Node**: `Wait 1s`
- **Function**: Adds a 1-second delay before proceeding to the next message in the loop (helps avoid Telegram rate limits and creates more natural pacing).

### ✅ 6. Optional: External API Request
- **Node**: `HTTP Request`
- **Function**: Makes an external API request to `https://devcodejourney.com/` (placeholder URL).
- **Note**: Currently not connected to message flow but can be used to fetch or send additional data.

### ✅ 7. Optional: Fetch Document from Google Docs
- **Node**: `Get a document in Google Docs`
- **Function**: Retrieves the contents of a Google Docs document from a provided link. Can be used to add static text, FAQs, or templates to the conversation.

### ✅ 8. Optional: Alternate Gemini Tool
- **Node**: `Message a model in Google Gemini`
- **Function**: Uses a different version (`gemini-2.0-flash-exp`) of the Gemini AI model for fast responses or testing purposes.

---

## 🔧 Technologies Used

- **Telegram Bot API** – for message triggers and replies  
- **Google Gemini (PaLM API)** – for AI-generated responses  
- **Google Docs API** – to fetch document content  
- **HTTP Request** – to connect to any third-party API  
- **n8n** – no-code/low-code workflow automation platform  

---

## ⚙️ Use Cases

- AI Chatbot for Telegram using Gemini  
- Personal Assistant or FAQ bot reading from Google Docs  
- Customer service bot that replies in natural language  
- Looping conversations with controlled pacing  
- External data fetching with AI processing  

---

## 📌 Notes

- Make sure you have the correct credentials configured in n8n for:
  - Telegram Bot
  - Google Docs OAuth2
  - Google Gemini API (PaLM)
- You can add error-handling nodes or conditionals to expand the logic.
- This can be extended into a full AI chatbot or customer support bot.

---

## 🧩 Nodes Used

| Node Name                          | Type                       | Purpose                                 |
|-----------------------------------|----------------------------|-----------------------------------------|
| Telegram Trigger                  | telegramTrigger            | Listens for incoming Telegram messages  |
| Loop Over Items                   | splitInBatches             | Handles batching of incoming data       |
| Message a model                   | langchain.googleGemini     | Sends prompt to Google Gemini (Pro)     |
| Send a text message               | telegram                   | Sends response back to Telegram user    |
| Wait 1s                           | wait                        | Adds a delay between responses          |
| HTTP Request                      | httpRequestTool            | Sends HTTP request to external API      |
| Get a document in Google Docs     | googleDocsTool             | Fetches content from Google Docs        |
| Message a model in Google Gemini | googleGeminiTool           | Alternate Gemini model for responses    |

---
